rulings.Case Title: ARNOLD S MALTER VS. JOAN MALTER OSBURN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney'S Fees And Costs On Appeal
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MARCH 28, 2008 LINE 2. DEFENDANT JOAN OSBURN'S Motion For Attorney'S Fees And Costs On Appeal GRANTED. Attorneys fees in the amount of $34,000 and costs in the amount of $632.99 are awarded. I have considered both the late filed Opposition and the Reply, and I have reviewed all the timesheets submitted by moving party. Moving party¿s rate of $250/hr. is reasonable. The time spent on the appeal is reasonable, and properly includes time participating in the Court of Appeal¿s mediation program as well as reviewing and understanding a related and consolidated appeal. Moving party¿s counsel prepared and argued a 56 page brief as well as a supplemental brief authored in response to a question from the Court of Appeal. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: MARLENE BARTON et al VS. ADVOCATE MINES LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MARCH 28, 2008 LINE 7. DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED as to causes of action for False Misrepresentation and as to Intentional Tort, as unopposed. GRANTED as to Punitive Damages. Plaintiff has no evidence that any officer, director or managing agent of the defendant acted with malice. CC § 3294(b); Cruz v. Homebase, 83 Cal.App.4th 160, 167 (2000). DENIED as to strict liability. Defendant fails to sustain burden on factual issue whether defendant was solely contractor. The evidence shows that defendant was both a manufacturer (Dick Depo. at 39; Trueblod Depo. at 2146) as well as contractor regarding asbestos products. Defendant¿s objections regarding the evidence of Dick and Trueblood are overruled. These are sworn statement equivalent to declarations, which are admissible for purposes of summary adjudication motions. GRANTED as to causes of action for False Misrepresentation and as to Intentional Tort, as unopposed. =(302/CK/VC)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL BERNARD SABOL JR VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MARCH 28, 2008 LINE 8. DEFENDANT SEARIVER MARITIME, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. Defendant has not sustained its burden to shifted the burden to plaintiff to defeat the motion. Andrews, 138 C.A.4th 96, 106-107 does not apply, for here there is no issue concerning failure to identify a manufacturer. Plaintiff¿s discovery responses are not ¿factually devoid¿ on the central issue posed by this motion, i.e., whether defendant acted with due care or reasonably. The material provided by defendant (Vickery¿s addresses) do not obviously relate to asbestos nor to its use in engine rooms, nor, more particularly, to the level of care due during the time the plaintiff was allegedly exposed--- some thirty years later after the speeches. Even if defendant had met its initial burden on this motion, Plaintiff has presented sufficient evidence of his likely contact with asbestos on the vessel in question, as well as evidence of knowledge of the dangers of asbestos at the relevant time (Cohen Dec. ¶ 7; Backenstoe Dec. EX J), and defendant¿s failure to warn. =(302/CK/PB)

rulings.Case Title: GREGORY J. HAMPTON VS. LAWYERS' MUTUAL INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Compel Arbitration And Request For Stay Of Proceedings;
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MARCH 28, 2008 LINE 12. DEFENDANT LAWYERS' MUTUAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S Petition To Compel Arbitration And Request For Stay Of Proceedings IS GRANTED. Motion to compel arbitration and stay, granted. Costs awarded in the amount of $785. The policy requires arbitration of coverage issues and issues of duty to defend are subsumed thereunder. This is manifested by the structure of Article 2 of the Policy. The duty to defend depends on the scope of coverage. Mansdorf is not on point. There the agreement expressly contemplated legal actions on some issues following earlier arbitration. Any ambiguities in the scope of arbitration are resolved in favor of coverage. Taylor v. Crane, 24 Cal.3d 442, 450, Weeks v. Crow, 113 Cal.App.3d 350, 353. =(302/CK/VC)

rulings.Case Title: PETER WONG VS. BRAD BISHOP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Ntc Of Mtn To Set Aside Entry Of Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MARCH 28, 2008 LINE 16. DEFENDANT BRAD BISHOP'S (Amended) MOTION To Set Aside Entry Of Default Judgment IS DENIED. Moving party failed to submit memorandum in support of motion. The motion is however decided here on the merits: (1) C.C.P § 473.5 does not apply because defendant had actual notice of the action in time to defend against it; (2) pursuant to C.C.P § 473 moving party made no evidentiary showing of excusable neglect, mistake, or other ground entitling him to relief. =(302/CK)
